value.found_quote: As a first step to mitigate incautious approvals, we shipped an OS-level sandbox (Seatbelt on macOS, bubblewrap on Linux) that hardens the boundary: reads are allowed, writes are allowed inside the workspace, but network is denied by default.
value.rationale: The page describes Claude Code's OS-level sandbox that constrains filesystem access for executed commands—reads allowed, writes restricted to the workspace—matching the claim of filesystem isolation. It also references "filesystem boundaries" and an open-sourced auditable runtime, indicating an active offering.
